free_text: Evaluated cross section and thick target yield data
bases of Zn+p processes for practical applications
CS-30 cyclotron.
Primary energy was 26 MeV for each of the sample
stacks.
Samples were prepared by electrolytic deposition of
isotopically enriched 66Zn (99%), 67Zn (91.5%) and
68Zn (98%) material onto Ni backing. Up to 8 samples
were stacked together with Cu and Ti foils used for
beam monitoring and Al foils used as beam energy
degraders.
Stacks were irradiated
for 30-70 minutes with 200-600 nano-a proton beam.
Beam was monitored by Faraday Cup and via monitor
reactions.
Beam energy at each Zn target was calculated using
range-energy formula of Williamson et al., CEA-R-3042,
Normal high resolution gamma-spectrometry
was applied.
Correction was made for dead time, pile-up and cascade
losses.
F.Tarkanyi et al., Appl.
O. Schwerer et al.,
INDC(NDS)-218 (1989) and H.Piel et al., Radiochem.
P.Kopecky et al., Appl.
Approved by S.Takacs, 15 Oct 1998.
TS
OS. Deleted sub.5 (duplication of sub.4)
Including direct formation of 65Zn and decay of 65Ga.
Details are given in F.Tarkanyi et al., Acta
Radiologica, Suppl. 376, 72 (1991).
Overall uncertainties in cross section were
estimated by summing up errors in current integration,
counting statistics, detector efficiency, number of
target nuclei and decay data.
Data taken from table 1 of main reference.
Details are given in F.Tarkanyi et al., Acta

Including direct formation of 65Zn and decay of 65Ga.
individual data sets were fit using a Cubic-
Spline method. Data are given at 0.5 MeV increments.
The associated error of a fitted data point was
estimated by summing up the error of the nearest
experimentel data point and the deviation of the
fitted cross section value from the value given by
linear interpolation of the two neighbouring
experimental data points at the fitted energy point.
Data taken from table 3 of main reference.
Recommended fitted experimental cross sections values
for calculating experimental data were taken from
This reference, and the following references:
M. Bonardi et al., ???? RC, 76, 311 (1983)
A. Hermanne et al., private communication (1994)
V.N. Levkovski, cross sections of.. (Inter-vesi,
Moscow, Russia, 1991)
F. Tarkanyi et al., Radiochem. Acta, 50, 19 (1990)
